deviations.description: The removal of the precondition based on 'donor.donation_count == 0' broadens the requirement to all donors, not just first-time donors. The process model on page 1 only requires providing information about the nature of blood donations to first-time donors. Therefore, the process does not provide this information to repeat donors, directly violating the stricter Version 2 requirement.
process_model_reference: Provide information of the nature of the blood donations

deviations.description: Changing the action dimension from 'data' to 'control_flow' does not affect compliance, as the process model already represents the sequence of activities rather than data conditions.
process_model_reference: Provide information of the nature of the blood donations

deviations.description: The removal of the data value condition (donor.donation_count == 0) means the obligation now applies to all donors. The process model only enforces this for first-time donors, resulting in a violation for repeat donors.
process_model_reference: Provide information of the nature of the blood donations

deviations.description: Switching the compliance pattern from 'precedence' to 'activity_occurs' does not introduce a stricter requirement, as the process model already ensures the activity occurs for first-time donors.
process_model_reference: Provide information of the nature of the blood donations

deviations.description: The new requirement obligates the activity 'provide_information_nature_blood_donations' for all donors, but the process model only includes this for first-time donors. This results in a direct violation for repeat donors.
process_model_reference: Provide information of the nature of the blood donations
